pi_items.metadata_value: Setup script references USSC FY2024 Sourcebook of Federal Sentencing Statistics for top offenses on supervised release: drug, fraud, theft, weapons
pi_items.verified_value: USSC FY2024 Sourcebook exists and confirms drug, fraud/theft, and firearms are among the most common federal offenses. USSC data shows drugs (~30% of cases), fraud/theft (~9% of cases), and firearms violations are primary offense categories.
pi_items.source: Web search: https://www.ussc.gov/research/sourcebook-2024 and https://www.ussc.gov/research/quick-facts/supervised-release confirmed these offense categories dominate federal caseload

pi_items.metadata_value: Setup script references 18 U.S.C. § 3563(b) as basis for standard supervision conditions
pi_items.verified_value: 18 U.S.C. § 3563(b) is a real federal statute governing discretionary conditions of probation, including standard supervision requirements
pi_items.source: Web search: https://www.law.cornell.edu/uscode/text/18/3563 confirmed this statute exists and authorizes standard conditions like reporting to probation officer

pi_items.metadata_value: Setup script references 18 U.S.C. § 1344 (Bank Fraud), § 924(c) (Firearms), § 1343 (Wire Fraud) as common supervised release offenses
pi_items.verified_value: All three federal offense statutes are real and commonly prosecuted: § 1344 covers bank fraud schemes, § 1343 covers wire fraud, § 924(c) covers firearms offenses in crime of violence/drug trafficking contexts
pi_items.source: Web searches confirmed all three statutes exist: https://www.law.cornell.edu/uscode/text/18/1344 (bank fraud), https://www.law.cornell.edu/uscode/text/18/1343 (wire fraud), https://www.law.cornell.edu/uscode/text/18/924 (firearms)

pi_items.metadata_value: Required note text references 'Policy CPO-7.3b' for mandatory monthly contact reporting escalation
pi_items.source: Exhaustive web searches for 'Policy CPO-7.3b', 'CPO-7.3b federal probation', and '"CPO-7.3b" probation' found no official federal policy documents or references with this designation. No results in USSC Guidelines Manual, U.S. Courts official documentation, or general web search.

privileged_info_summary: This is a synthetic probation caseload triage task for benchmarking. The task correctly defines 3 non-compliant cases (last contact before 2025-12-01: Williams 2025-09-14, Gutierrez 2025-10-02, Reed 2025-08-30) and 4 compliant cases (last contact after 2025-12-01: Osei 2026-01-15, Nair 2026-01-22, Foster 2026-02-05, Belobrov 2026-02-18). All metadata claims in task.json are verified correct. The task references real federal statutes (18 U.S.C. §§ 3563(b), 1343, 1344, 924(c)) and the actual USSC FY2024 Sourcebook which documents drug, fraud, and firearms as top federal offenses. The specific policy reference 'CPO-7.3b' cannot be verified in official federal probation documentation but is part of the required note text agents must add. Data is entirely synthetic (de-identified case names, setup via script).
